web_text:
The beginning of the Good News of Jesus Christ, the Son of God. As it is written in the prophets,
“Behold, I send my messenger before your face,
who will prepare your way before you: the voice of one crying in the wilderness,
‘Make ready the way of the Lord!
Make his paths straight!’” John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching the baptism of repentance for forgiveness of sins.
slang_text:
This is the start of the epic Good News about Jesus Christ, the Son of God, for real. Just like it's written down by the prophets:
"Check it -- I'm sending my messenger ahead of you to clear the way,
a dude shouting out in the middle of nowhere,
'Get the Lord's road ready!
Make them paths straight, no detours!'" John rolled up in the wilderness dunking people and preaching that you gotta repent and get baptized so your sins get wiped clean.

web_text: All the country of Judea and all those of Jerusalem went out to him. They were baptized by him in the Jordan river, confessing their sins. John was clothed with camel’s hair and a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey. He preached, saying, “After me comes he who is mightier than I, the thong of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and loosen. I baptized you in water, but he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.”
slang_text: Everybody from Judea and Jerusalem was bailing out to see this guy, getting dunked in the Jordan River, spilling their sins out loud. John was rocking a camel-hair getup with a leather belt, chowing down on locusts and wild honey -- grody, but hey, that's John for you. He preached it loud: "There's a dude coming after me who's way mightier than I am -- I'm not even worthy to untie his sandals, for real. I've been dunking you in water, but he's gonna baptize you in the Holy Spirit, and that's no joke."

web_text: In those days, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan. Immediately coming up from the water, he saw the heavens parting, and the Spirit descending on him like a dove. A voice came out of the sky, “You are my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.” Immediately the Spirit drove him out into the wilderness.
slang_text: Back in those days, Jesus bounced from Nazareth in Galilee and got baptized by John in the Jordan. The second he came up outta the water, boom -- the sky totally split open and he saw the Spirit coming down on him like a dove. A voice boomed out of the sky: "You're my beloved Son, and dude, you make me stoked." Immediately the Spirit shoved him straight out into the wilderness, no chill about it.

web_text:
He was there in the wilderness forty days tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals; and the angels were serving him. Now after John was taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the Good News of God’s Kingdom, and saying,
“The time is fulfilled, and God’s Kingdom is at hand! Repent, and believe in the Good News.” Passing along by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew the brother of Simon casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
slang_text:
He was out there in the wilderness forty days straight, getting tempted by Satan the whole time. Hanging with the wild animals, but the angels were taking care of him, so it wasn't all bad. After John got locked up, Jesus bounced into Galilee preaching the Good News of God's Kingdom, saying,
"The time's here, dudes -- God's Kingdom is totally at hand! Repent and believe the Good News, for real." Cruising along the Sea of Galilee, he spotted Simon and his brother Andrew tossing a net into the water, 'cause they were fishermen by trade.

web_text:
Jesus said to them,
“Come after me, and I will make you into fishers for men.” Immediately they left their nets, and followed him. Going on a little further from there, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John, his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets. Immediately he called them, and they left their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired servants, and went after him.
slang_text:
Jesus said to them,
"Come after me, and I'll turn you into fishers of men instead." Immediately they dropped their nets and bounced with him, no questions asked. Walking a little further, he spotted James, son of Zebedee, and his brother John, mending nets in the boat. Immediately he called them too, and they left their dad Zebedee in the boat with the hired hands and took off after Jesus like it was no big deal.

web_text: They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath day he entered into the synagogue and taught. They were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as having authority, and not as the scribes. Immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out, saying, “Ha! What do we have to do with you, Jesus, you Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”
slang_text: They rolled into Capernaum, and right on the Sabbath he headed into the synagogue and started teaching. Everybody was totally blown away 'cause he taught like he actually had authority, not bogus like the scribes. Immediately some dude in there with an unclean spirit starts screaming, "Ha! What's your damage with us, Jesus of Nazareth? You here to wreck us? I know exactly who you are: the Holy One of God!"

web_text:
Jesus rebuked him, saying,
“Be quiet, and come out of him!” The unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him. They were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching? For with authority he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him!” The report of him went out immediately everywhere into all the region of Galilee and its surrounding area.
slang_text:
Jesus shut him down hard, saying,
"Zip it, and get outta him!" The unclean spirit threw the guy into convulsions, let out a huge scream, and bounced right outta there. Everyone was totally freaked, asking each other, "What is this? Some radical new teaching? He's straight-up bossing unclean spirits and they obey him, no duh they're scared!" Word about him spread immediately, everywhere, all across Galilee and beyond.

web_text: Immediately, when they had come out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John. Now Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick with a fever, and immediately they told him about her. He came and took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them. At evening, when the sun had set, they brought to him all who were sick, and those who were possessed by demons.
slang_text: The second they bailed out of the synagogue, they headed to Simon and Andrew's crib, James and John tagging along. Simon's mother-in-law was laid out sick with a fever, and immediately they told Jesus about her. He came in, grabbed her hand, and pulled her up -- the fever bounced instantly and she got up and started serving everybody, like a boss. When the sun went down that evening, folks dragged in everybody who was sick or demon-possessed.

web_text: All the city was gathered together at the door. He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons. He didn’t allow the demons to speak, because they knew him. Early in the morning, while it was still dark, he rose up and went out, and departed into a deserted place, and prayed there. Simon and those who were with him followed after him;
slang_text: The whole city was crammed at the door, no joke. He healed a ton of people with all kinds of grody diseases and cast out a bunch of demons. He wouldn't let the demons talk, 'cause they knew exactly who he was. Super early, still pitch dark out, he got up and bounced to a deserted spot to pray by himself. Simon and his crew went hunting for him;

web_text:
and they found him, and told him, “Everyone is looking for you.” He said to them,
“Let’s go elsewhere into the next towns, that I may preach there also, because I came out for this reason.” He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out demons. A leper came to him, begging him, kneeling down to him, and saying to him, “If you want to, you can make me clean.”
slang_text:
and they found him, telling him, "Everybody's looking for you, dude!" He said to them,
"Let's bounce to the next towns instead, so I can preach there too -- that's the whole reason I came out here." So he went through their synagogues all across Galilee, preaching and casting out demons like it was nothing. Then a leper came up to him, begging, dropping to his knees, saying, "If you're willing, you can totally make me clean."

web_text:
Being moved with compassion, he stretched out his hand, and touched him, and said to him,
“I want to. Be made clean.” When he had said this, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was made clean. He strictly warned him, and immediately sent him out, and said to him,
“See you say nothing to anybody, but go show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing the things which Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”
slang_text:
Moved to compassion, Jesus reached out his hand and touched the guy, saying,
"I'm willing. Be clean." The second he said it, the leprosy bounced -- totally gone, the guy was clean. Jesus gave him a stern warning and sent him packing right away, saying,
"Don't say a word to anybody about this, but go show yourself to the priest and offer up what Moses commanded for your cleansing, so it's proof for everybody."

web_text: But he went out, and began to proclaim it much, and to spread about the matter, so that Jesus could no more openly enter into a city, but was outside in desert places: and they came to him from everywhere.
slang_text: But the guy couldn't keep his mouth shut -- he went out blabbing about it everywhere, spreading the word so much that Jesus couldn't even roll into a city out in the open anymore. He had to lay low in deserted spots instead, and still people came at him from everywhere, no lie.

web_text: When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was in the house. Immediately many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even around the door; and he spoke the word to them. Four people came, carrying a paralytic to him. When they could not come near to him for the crowd, they removed the roof where he was. When they had broken it up, they let down the mat that the paralytic was lying on.
slang_text: So Jesus rolls back into Capernaum after being outta town a few days, and word gets out he's chillin' in a house somewhere. No joke, so many people packed in there wasn't even room by the door, and he's kicking it to the crowd, teachin' the word. Then these four dudes show up, hauling a paralyzed guy on a mat. But the crowd's so gnarly thick they can't even get near the door, so they climb up top, bust a hole clean through the roof, and lower the guy down on his mat right in front of Jesus. Totally bold move.

web_text:
Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic,
“Son, your sins are forgiven you.” But there were some of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts, “Why does this man speak blasphemies like that? Who can forgive sins but God alone?” Immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, said to them,
“Why do you reason these things in your hearts?
slang_text: Jesus clocks their faith and tells the paralyzed dude, "Buddy, your sins are forgiven, fer sure." But some scribes sittin' there start trippin' in their heads, thinkin', "Whoa, harsh -- who does this guy think he is, talking blasphemy like that? Only God can forgive sins, no duh." Jesus reads their vibe right away and calls 'em out: "Why you dipsticks stewin' on that in your hearts?

web_text: Which is easier, to tell the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Arise, and take up your bed, and walk?’ But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic— “I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.” He arose, and immediately took up the mat, and went out in front of them all; so that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”
slang_text: Which is easier -- telling the paralyzed dude 'Your sins are forgiven,' or saying 'Get up, grab your mat, and walk'? But so you know for real the Son of Man's got the authority right here on earth to forgive sins" -- and he turns to the paralytic -- "I'm tellin' you, get up, grab your mat, and bounce home." Dude gets right up, grabs the mat like it's nothin', and walks out in front of everybody. The whole crowd's totally freaking out, praising God and going, "No way! We have never seen anything this epic!"

web_text:
He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them. As he passed by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax office, and he said to him,
“Follow me.” And he arose and followed him. He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him. The scribes and the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why is it that he eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”
slang_text: Jesus bounces back out to the seaside, and the whole crowd swarms him again, so he starts teachin'. Walking along, he spots Levi, Alphaeus's kid, posted up at the tax booth, and just says, "Follow me." No hesitation -- dude gets up and follows him, just like that. Later Jesus is chillin' at the table in Levi's crib, and a bunch of tax collectors and other sinners pull up a seat with him and his disciples -- turns out there's a whole crew of 'em following him around. The scribes and Pharisees catch wind he's eating with that crowd and get all bent outta shape, asking his disciples, "What's his deal, eating and drinking with tax collectors and sinners like that?"

web_text:
When Jesus heard it, he said to them,
“Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.” John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?” Jesus said to them,
“Can the groomsmen fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can’t fast. But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then will they fast in that day.
slang_text: Jesus hears this and hits back, "Healthy people don't need no doctor -- it's the sick ones that do. I didn't roll up to call the righteous, I came for the sinners, to get 'em turned around." Meanwhile John's disciples and the Pharisees are out there fasting, and some folks come ask Jesus, "Why do John's disciples and the Pharisees' crew fast, but yours don't fast at all?" Jesus tells 'em, "Can the groom's buddies fast while the groom's still right there at the party? No way, dude, not while he's around. But there's a day comin' when the groom gets taken away from 'em -- that's when they'll fast, for real.

web_text: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, or else the patch shrinks and the new tears away from the old, and a worse hole is made. No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine pours out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins.” He was going on the Sabbath day through the grain fields, and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of grain. The Pharisees said to him, “Behold, why do they do that which is not lawful on the Sabbath day?”
slang_text: Nobody in their right mind sews a patch of unshrunk cloth onto a raggedy old garment -- the new patch shrinks up and rips clean away from the old, and you get a worse hole than you started with, way harsh. And nobody pours fresh wine into old, brittle wineskins either, or the new wine's gonna bust 'em wide open and you lose the wine and the skins both -- total bogus waste. No, you pour fresh wine into fresh skins." So Jesus is walkin' through the grain fields on the Sabbath, and his disciples start pluckin' some heads of grain as they go. The Pharisees jump on it: "Check it out -- why they doin' something that's against the rules on the Sabbath?"

web_text:
He said to them,
“Did you never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry—he, and those who were with him? How he entered into God’s house at the time of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the show bread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those who were with him?” He said to them,
“The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath. Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”
slang_text: Jesus tells 'em, "You dudes ever read what David did when he and his crew got hungry and needed grub? How he waltzed into God's house, back when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the show bread -- which no duh, only the priests are supposed to eat -- and even passed it out to his guys?" Then he lays it on 'em straight: "The Sabbath got made for people, not the other way around. So chill out -- the Son of Man's the boss even over the Sabbath, fer sure."

web_text:
He entered again into the synagogue, and there was a man there who had his hand withered. They watched him, whether he would heal him on the Sabbath day, that they might accuse him. He said to the man who had his hand withered,
“Stand up.” He said to them,
“Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?” But they were silent.
slang_text: So Jesus bounces back into the synagogue, and there's this dude with a totally withered hand just sitting there. The religious dudes are watching him like hawks, hoping he'll heal on the Sabbath so they can nail him for it -- straight up bogus setup. Jesus tells the guy with the bum hand, "Get up here." Then he turns to the crowd, "Yo, is it cool to do good on the Sabbath, or is doing harm the play? Savin' a life or takin' one -- which is it?" Nobody said a word. Talk to the hand, dudes, 'cause they had nothin'.

web_text:
When he had looked around at them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their hearts, he said to the man,
“Stretch out your hand.”
 He stretched it out, and his hand was restored as healthy as the other. The Pharisees went out, and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him. Jesus withdrew to the sea with his disciples, and a great multitude followed him from Galilee, from Judea, from Jerusalem, from Idumaea, beyond the Jordan, and those from around Tyre and Sidon. A great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came to him.
slang_text: Jesus scoped out the whole room, totally steamed, but way harsh bummed that their hearts were so hardened up. He looks at the guy and goes, "Stick your hand out." The dude stretched it out and boom -- healed up righteous, good as the other one. The Pharisees stormed out and immediately started scheming with the Herodians on how to take Jesus down, which is grody behavior for religious types. Jesus and his crew bailed for the sea, and this massive crowd tagged along from Galilee, Judea, Jerusalem, Idumaea, past the Jordan, even from Tyre and Sidon way. Word of his epic miracles had spread, and everybody wanted in.

web_text: He spoke to his disciples that a little boat should stay near him because of the crowd, so that they wouldn’t press on him. For he had healed many, so that as many as had diseases pressed on him that they might touch him. The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, “You are the Son of God!” He sternly warned them that they should not make him known.
slang_text: He told his crew to keep a little boat on standby near the shore so the mob wouldn't crush him -- dude had healed so many people that everybody with a disease was straight-up mobbing him just to get a touch. Even the unclean spirits, whenever they clocked him, would drop down and scream, "You're the Son of God!" But Jesus laid down the law hard, telling them not to blow his cover.

web_text: He went up into the mountain, and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they went to him. He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach, and to have authority to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);
slang_text: Jesus headed up the mountain and called over the dudes he actually wanted, and they came running. He picked out twelve to roll with him full-time, so he could send 'em out to preach and give 'em the power to heal the sick and cast out demons -- like a boss. First up, Simon, who he renamed Peter.

web_text:
James the son of Zebedee; and John, the brother of James, (whom he called Boanerges, which means, Sons of Thunder); Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot; and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.
Then he came into a house. The multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
slang_text: Then there's James, son of Zebedee, and his brother John -- Jesus tagged those two "Sons of Thunder," which is a hella righteous nickname. Then Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, the wastoid who'd end up selling Jesus out. After that Jesus went home, and the crowd packed in so thick again that nobody could even grab a bite to eat.

web_text:
When his friends heard it, they went out to seize him: for they said, “He is insane.” The scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul,” and, “By the prince of the demons he casts out the demons.” He summoned them, and said to them in parables,
“How can Satan cast out Satan? If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
slang_text: When his own people heard about it, they went out to grab him, all "he's totally lost it." Meanwhile the scribes down from Jerusalem were trippin' way harder, saying, "He's got Beelzebul in him," and, "He's only bootin' out demons 'cause he's running with the demon prince himself." Bogus accusation, but Jesus called 'em over and hit 'em with some parables. "Yo, how's Satan supposed to kick out Satan? That's trippin' logic. If a kingdom turns on itself, that kingdom's toast, no duh."

web_text: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand. If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can’t stand, but has an end. But no one can enter into the house of the strong man to plunder, unless he first binds the strong man; and then he will plunder his house. Most certainly I tell you, all sins of the descendants of man will be forgiven, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;
slang_text: "Same deal with a house divided against itself -- that house is goin' down, no way it stands. And if Satan's out here fighting himself, he's toast too, it's over for him, fer sure. Nobody just waltzes into a strong dude's crib and jacks his stuff -- you gotta tie the guy up first, then you can raid the place like a boss. For real, I'm tellin' you straight: every sin humans commit can get forgiven, even all the trash talk and blasphemin' they spew;"

web_text: but whoever may blaspheme against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation.” —because they said, “He has an unclean spirit.” His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him. A multitude was sitting around him, and they told him, “Behold, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters are outside looking for you.”
slang_text: "But whoever trash-talks the Holy Spirit is never gettin' forgiven -- that's a bogus deal locked in for eternity." He said all that 'cause they'd been claiming he had an unclean spirit, what's your damage, dudes. Then his mom and his brothers showed up, standing outside, and sent word in to call him out. There was a whole crowd chillin' around him, and they told him, "Yo, check it -- your mom and your brothers and sisters are outside looking for you."

web_text:
He answered them,
“Who are my mother and my brothers?” Looking around at those who sat around him, he said,
“Behold, my mother and my brothers! For whoever does the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.”
slang_text: He came back at 'em with, "Who's my real mother and brothers, anyway?" Then he looked around at the crew sitting there and goes, "Check it out -- these are my mother and brothers! 'Cause whoever does what God wants, that person's my brother, my sister, and my mother, for real."

web_text:
Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea. He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching, “Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow, and as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds
came and devoured it.
slang_text: So he's chillin' on the water again, teachin' by the beach, and the crowd's so hella huge he's gotta hop in a boat just to have some room, while everybody else stands on the shore like an audience. He starts droppin' knowledge in parables, and he's like, "Listen up, dudes! Check it — this farmer heads out to plant some seeds, and while he's tossin' 'em around, some land right on the road, and boom, the birds swoop in and scarf 'em down."

web_text: Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil. When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away. Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit. Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some produced thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much.”
slang_text: "Other seeds hit the rocky ground, where there's barely any dirt, so they sprout up quick — but 'cause there's no depth to put down roots, the sun comes up and totally scorches 'em, and they wither away, grody. Other seeds land in the thorns, and the thorns grow up and choke the life outta 'em, no fruit, bogus. But some seeds hit the good dirt, and dude, they blow up — thirty times, sixty times, even a hundred times what got planted. Totally tubular harvest."

web_text:
He said,
“Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.” When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables. He said to them,
“To you is given the mystery of God’s Kingdom, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables, that ‘seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.’”
slang_text: He goes, "If you got ears, dude, use 'em." Later, when it's just him and his crew — the twelve plus some others hangin' around — they ask him what's the deal with all these parables. He tells 'em, "You guys get let in on the secret of God's Kingdom, no duh, but for the outsiders it's all riddles, so that 'they can look right at it and not see it, hear it and not get it, or else they might turn back around and get their sins wiped clean.'"

web_text:
He said to them,
“Don’t you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables? The farmer sows the word. The ones by the road are the ones where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them. These in the same way are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.
slang_text: Then he tells 'em, "You don't get this parable? How you gonna get any of 'em, then? The farmer's plantin' the word, fer sure. The seeds on the road are the folks who hear the word, but Satan swoops in right away like those birds and snatches it outta 'em before it even sinks in. Then there's the ones on the rocky ground — they hear the word and totally geek out over it, all stoked right away."

web_text: They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble. Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word, and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful. Those which were sown on the good ground are those who hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times.”
slang_text: "But they got no roots, so it don't last — soon as trouble or hassles come 'cause of the word, they bail, like a bunch of wastoids. Then there's the ones sown in the thorns — they hear the word, but stressin' about this life, gettin' greedy for cash, and craving all kinds of stuff chokes it right out, and nothin' comes of it, total bummer. But the ones on the good ground, they hear the word, they grab onto it, and they bear fruit — thirty times, sixty times, a hundred times over. Righteous."

web_text:
He said to them,
“Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket

or under a bed? Isn’t it put on a stand? For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light. If any man has ears to hear, let him hear.” He said to them,
“Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.
slang_text: He tells 'em, "You don't light a lamp just to shove it under a basket or stuff it under the bed, right? As if. You put it up on a stand where it can shine. There's nothin' hidden that ain't gonna come out into the open eventually — nothin' secret that won't get exposed. If you got ears, dude, use 'em." Then he goes, "Pay attention to what you're hearin'. Whatever you dish out, that's what's comin' back at you — and then some, 'cause the ones who really listen get even more."

web_text:
For whoever has, to him will more be given, and he who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.” He said,
“God’s Kingdom is as if a man should cast seed on the earth, and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, though he doesn’t know how. For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.

web_text:
When he had entered into a house away from the multitude, his disciples asked him about the parable. He said to them,
“Are you also without understanding? Don’t you perceive that whatever goes into the man from outside can’t defile him, because it doesn’t go into his heart, but into his stomach, then into the latrine, thus purifying all foods?” He said,
“That which proceeds out of the man, that defiles the man.
slang_text:
Once he ditched the crowd and got inside a house, his disciples asked him what he meant by that whole riddle. He goes,
"Are you spazzin' out too? Don't you get it -- whatever goes into a guy from outside can't make him defiled, 'cause it doesn't touch his heart, it just passes through his stomach and out into the latrine." (Basically he was saying all food's fair game, no duh.) Then he said,
"It's what comes outta a person, that's what makes him grody.

web_text: For from within, out of the hearts of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, sexual sins, murders, thefts, covetings, wickedness, deceit, lustful desires, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, and foolishness. All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.” From there he arose, and went away into the borders of Tyre and Sidon. He entered into a house, and didn’t want anyone to know it, but he couldn’t escape notice.
slang_text: 'Cause it's from inside, straight outta a guy's heart, that all the bogus stuff comes crawling out: evil thoughts, cheating on your spouse, sexual sins, murder, stealing, greed, wickedness, lying, nasty desires, a jealous eye, trash-talking God, pride, straight-up foolishness. All that grody junk comes from the inside, and that's what defiles a person." From there he took off and headed into the territory around Tyre and Sidon. He ducked into a house, tryin' to keep it on the down-low, but no way was he gonna stay hidden.

web_text:
For a woman, whose little daughter had an unclean spirit, having heard of him, came and fell down at his feet. Now the woman was a Greek, a Syrophoenician by race. She begged him that he would cast the demon out of her daughter. But Jesus said to her,
“Let the children be filled first, for it is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.” But she answered him, “Yes, Lord. Yet even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”
slang_text:
See, there's this woman whose little girl had a demon botherin' her, and word got to her about Jesus. She showed up, dropped right at his feet, totally begging. Now this Betty was Greek, a Syrophoenician by birth, and she pleaded with him to cast that demon outta her daughter. Jesus told her,
"Let the kids eat first -- it ain't right to take the children's bread and toss it to the dogs." But she came right back at him, no fear, "Fer sure, Lord. But even the dogs under the table get to eat the crumbs the kids drop."

web_text:
He said to her,

Again he departed from the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and came to the sea of Galilee, through the middle of the region of Decapolis. They brought to him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech. They begged him to lay his hand on him.
slang_text:
He told her,

Some folks brought him a guy who was deaf and could hardly talk right, and they begged Jesus to just lay a hand on him.

web_text:
He took him aside from the multitude, privately, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat, and touched his tongue. Looking up to heaven, he sighed, and said to him,
“Ephphatha!”
 that is,
“Be opened!” Immediately his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was released, and he spoke clearly. He commanded them that they should tell no one, but the more he commanded them, so much the more widely they proclaimed it.
slang_text:
He pulled the guy away from the crowd, just the two of them, stuck his fingers in the guy's ears, spat, and touched his tongue. Then he looked up to the sky, let out a heavy sigh, and said,

"Open up!" Bam, right then his ears popped open, his tongue got loose, and he was talkin' clear as day. Jesus told everybody to keep it zipped and not tell a soul, but the more he told 'em to chill, the more they blabbed it all over the place.

web_text: They were astonished beyond measure, saying, “He has done all things well. He makes even the deaf hear, and the mute speak!”
slang_text: People were blown away beyond belief, saying, "This dude does everything totally right. He's even got the deaf hearing and the mute talking -- straight-up epic!"

His disciples came back with, "Dude, where are we even supposed to score bread out here in the middle of nowhere?"

in that unquenchable fire, 'where their worm never dies, and the fire never burns out.'

of fire, ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’

so that they are no longer two, but one flesh.

What therefore God has joined together, let no man separate.” In the house, his disciples asked him again about the same matter. He said to them,

web_text:
As he was going out into the way, one ran to him, knelt before him, and asked him, “Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?” Jesus said to him,
“Why do you call me good? No one is good except one—God. You know the commandments: ‘Do not murder,’ ‘Do not commit adultery,’ ‘Do not steal,’ ‘Do not give false testimony,’ ‘Do not defraud,’ ‘Honor your father and mother.’” He said to him, “Teacher, I have observed all these things from my youth.”
slang_text: As Jesus was headin' out, this dude comes sprinting up, drops to his knees, and goes, "Good Teacher, what do I gotta do to score eternal life?" Jesus was like, "Why you callin' me good? Nobody's good except God, no duh. You know the commandments -- don't murder, don't cheat, don't steal, don't lie on the stand, don't rip people off, honor your mom and dad." The guy goes, "Teacher, I've kept every one of those since I was a little kid."

web_text:
Jesus looking at him loved him, and said to him,
“One thing you lack. Go, sell whatever you have, and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me, taking up the cross.” But his face fell at that saying, and he went away sorrowful, for he was one who had great possessions. Jesus looked around, and said to his disciples,
“How difficult it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!” The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again,
“Children, how hard is it for those who trust in riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!
slang_text: Jesus looked at the guy and totally loved him, straight up, and said, "You're missin' one thing. Go sell everything you own, give the cash to people who need it, and you'll bank yourself some serious treasure in heaven. Then come follow me, cross and all." But the dude's face just fell -- bogus news for him -- and he bounced outta there bummed 'cause he was loaded. Jesus looked around at his crew and goes, "Man, it's hella hard for rich folks to get into God's Kingdom!" The disciples were straight-up shocked at that. But Jesus said it again, "Dudes, it's rough for anybody who trusts their cash to get into God's Kingdom!

web_text:
It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.” They were exceedingly astonished, saying to him, “Then who can be saved?” Jesus, looking at them, said,
“With men it is impossible, but not with God, for all things are possible with God.” Peter began to tell him, “Behold, we have left all, and have followed you.”
slang_text: "It's easier for a camel to squeeze through a needle's eye than for some rich dude to get into God's Kingdom." That blew their minds even more, and they go, "Then who in the world CAN get saved?" Jesus looked at 'em and said, "No way can people pull that off on their own -- but with God? Anything's possible, for real, all things." Peter pipes up, "Check it, we bailed on everything to follow you."

web_text:
Jesus said,
“Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or land, for my sake, and for the sake of the Good News, but he will receive one hundred times more now in this time, houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and land, with persecutions; and in the age to come eternal life. But many who are first will be last; and the last first.” They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus was going in front of them, and they were amazed; and those who followed were afraid. He again took the twelve, and began to tell them the things that were going to happen to him.
slang_text: Jesus said, "Straight up, I'm tellin' you -- nobody who's ditched house, brothers, sisters, mom, dad, kids, or land for me and for the Good News is gonna miss out. They'll score a hundred times more, right here, right now -- houses, family, land, the whole bit, plus persecution thrown in for free -- and in the age comin' up, eternal life. But heads up, a bunch of folks who are first now are gonna end up last, and the last will be first." They were on the road up to Jerusalem, Jesus walkin' out front, and the crew was totally freaked out, the ones followin' were scared outta their minds. He pulled the twelve aside again and started layin' out what was about to go down to him.

web_text:
“Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death, and will deliver him to the Gentiles. They will mock him, spit on him, scourge him, and kill him. On the third day he will rise again.” James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came near to him, saying, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we will ask.” He said to them,
“What do you want me to do for you?”
slang_text: "Check it -- we're headed up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man's gonna get handed over to the chief priests and scribes. They'll sentence him to death and dump him off to the Gentiles, who'll mock him, spit on him, whip him, and kill him. But three days later he's comin' back to life." Then James and John, Zebedee's boys, sidle up and go, "Teacher, we want you to do whatever we ask." He goes, "So what's the ask?"

web_text:
They said to him, “Grant to us that we may sit, one at your right hand, and one at your left hand, in your glory.” But Jesus said to them,
“You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?” They said to him, “We are able.”
Jesus said to them,
“You shall indeed drink the cup that I drink, and you shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with; but to sit at my right hand and at my left hand is not mine to give, but for whom it has been prepared.”
slang_text: They said, "Let us sit next to you in your glory -- one on your right, one on your left." But Jesus goes, "You don't even know what you're askin', dudes. Can you drink outta the same cup I'm drinkin', get dunked in the same baptism I'm gettin' dunked in?" They said, "Fer sure, we can." Jesus told 'em, "You will drink my cup, alright, and you'll get that baptism too. But sittin' at my right or left hand ain't mine to hand out like party favors -- that's already been decided for whoever it's meant for."

web_text:
When the ten heard it, they began to be indignant towards James and John. Jesus summoned them, and said to them,
“You know that they who are recognized as rulers over the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them. But it shall not be so among you, but whoever wants to become great among you shall be your servant. Whoever of you wants to become first among you, shall be bondservant of all.
slang_text: When the other ten heard about it, they got hella ticked at James and John. Jesus called everybody together and told 'em, "You know how the big shots who run the nations lord it over everybody, and their VIPs push people around. Well that's not how it's gonna roll with you. Whoever wants to be a big deal among you has gotta be everybody's servant. Whoever wants to be number one has gotta be a slave to the whole crew.

web_text: For the Son of Man also came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.” They came to Jericho. As he went out from Jericho, with his disciples and a great multitude, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the road. When he heard that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out, and say, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!” Many rebuked him, that he should be quiet, but he cried out much more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”
slang_text: "'Cause even the Son of Man didn't show up to get served -- he came to serve, and to give up his own life as a ransom for a whole mess of people." Then they hit Jericho, and as Jesus was bouncin' outta town with his disciples and a huge crowd, this blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus's kid, was sittin' by the road. Soon as he heard it was Jesus of Nazareth, he started hollerin', "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!" A bunch of people told him to zip it, but he just yelled even louder, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"

web_text:
Jesus stood still, and said,
“Call him.”
They called the blind man, saying to him, “Cheer up! Get up. He is calling you!” He, casting away his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus. Jesus asked him,
“What do you want me to do for you?”
The blind man said to him, “Rabboni, that I may see again.” Jesus said to him,
“Go your way. Your faith has made you well.”
 Immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus on the way.
slang_text: Jesus stopped in his tracks and goes, "Call him over." So they called the blind guy, tellin' him, "Cheer up, get up, he's callin' for you!" He whipped off his cloak, jumped up like a spaz with excitement, and came straight to Jesus. Jesus asked him, "What do you want me to do for you?" The blind man goes, "Rabboni, I just wanna see again." Jesus told him, "Go on, your faith healed you." Boom -- right then and there he could see, and he followed Jesus on down the road. Totally epic.

web_text:
When they came near to Jerusalem, to Bethsphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples, and said to them,
“Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him, and bring him. If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord needs him;’ and immediately he will send him back here.” They went away, and found a young donkey tied at the door outside in the open street, and they untied him.
slang_text: As they got close to Jerusalem, cruisin' up to Bethphage and Bethany near the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of his dudes ahead and told 'em, "Go check out that village right there. Soon as you roll in, you'll spot a young donkey tied up, one that nobody's ever ridden. Untie it and bring it back. If some spazoid asks 'What's your damage, why you jackin' that donkey?' just say, 'The Lord needs it,' and he'll send it back pronto." So they bounced, found the donkey tied up outside on the street just like Jesus called it, and untied it, no doubt.

web_text: Some of those who stood there asked them, “What are you doing, untying the young donkey?” They said to them just as Jesus had said, and they let them go. They brought the young donkey to Jesus, and threw their garments on it, and Jesus sat on it. Many spread their garments on the way, and others were cutting down branches from the trees, and spreading them on the road.
slang_text: Some randoms standing around were like, "Hey, what's your damage, untying that donkey?" So the disciples told 'em exactly what Jesus said, and the dudes let 'em roll. They brought the donkey to Jesus, threw their jackets over it like a boss, and Jesus hopped on. Tons of people spread their coats on the road, and other dudes were cutting branches off the trees to lay down too -- total red-carpet treatment.

web_text:
Those who went in front, and those who followed, cried out, “Hosanna!
 Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!” Jesus entered into the temple in Jerusalem. When he had looked around at everything, it being now evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve. The next day, when they had come out from Bethany, he was hungry.
slang_text: The whole crowd, front and back, was shoutin' their faces off, "Hosanna! Righteous is the dude who comes in the name of the Lord! Righteous is our main man David's kingdom, comin' in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest, totally!" It was pure cowabunga energy. Jesus rolled into the temple in Jerusalem, checked out the whole scene, and since it was gettin' late he bounced back to Bethany with the twelve. Next day, headin' out from Bethany, he was starvin'.

web_text:
Seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came to see if perhaps he might find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs. Jesus told it,
“May no one ever eat fruit from you again!”
 and his disciples heard it. They came to Jerusalem, and Jesus entered into the temple, and began to throw out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables, and the seats of those who sold the doves. He would not allow anyone to carry a container through the temple.
slang_text: Off in the distance Jesus spotted a fig tree all leafed out, so he went to check if it had any figs on it. But when he got there, nothin' but leaves -- bogus, 'cause it wasn't even fig season. So Jesus told the tree, "No dude's ever eatin' fruit off you again!" and his disciples heard him say it. They got to Jerusalem, and Jesus stormed into the temple and started flippin' out on the buyers and sellers -- like, straight-up wrecking the money-changers' tables and the dove-sellers' seats. He wasn't lettin' anybody haul stuff through the temple either. Way harsh, but he meant business.

web_text:
He taught, saying to them,
“Isn’t it written, ‘My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations?’
But you have made it a den of robbers!” The chief priests and the scribes heard it, and sought how they might destroy him. For they feared him, because all the multitude was astonished at his teaching. When evening came, he went out of the city. As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away from the roots.
slang_text: He laid it down for 'em, "Ain't it written, 'My house is s'posed to be called a house of prayer for every nation on the map?' But you dipsticks turned it into a hideout for crooks!" The chief priests and scribes caught wind of this and started plottin' how to take him out, 'cause they were scared stiff of him -- the whole crowd was totally blown away by his teaching. When it got dark, he split the city. Walkin' by the next morning, they saw that fig tree shriveled up dead, roots and all.

web_text:
Peter, remembering, said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree which you cursed has withered away.” Jesus answered them,
“Have faith in God. For most certainly I tell you, whoever may tell this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ and doesn’t doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says is happening; he shall have whatever he says. Therefore I tell you, all things whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you have received them, and you shall have them.
slang_text: Peter remembered and goes, "Rabbi, check it out! That fig tree you dissed is totally dried up!" Jesus came back with, "Have faith in God, for real. I'm tellin' you straight up, whoever tells this mountain, 'Get up and chuck yourself in the ocean,' and doesn't chicken out but believes it's actually gonna go down -- it'll happen for him, no doubt. So I'm tellin' you, whatever you pray for and ask, believe you already got it, and it's yours."

web_text: Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father, who is in heaven, may also forgive you your transgressions. But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions.” They came again to Jerusalem, and as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders came to him, and they began saying to him, “By what authority do you do these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?”
slang_text: "And whenever you're prayin', forgive folks if you got beef with anybody, so your Father up in heaven forgives your junk too. But if you don't forgive, your Father ain't gonna forgive your junk either -- so don't be a spaz about it." They rolled back into Jerusalem, and while Jesus was walkin' through the temple, the chief priests, scribes, and elders swarmed him and started in, "Says who? What's your damage comin' in here doing all this? Who gave you the green light?"

web_text:
Jesus said to them,
“I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things. The baptism of John—was it from heaven, or from men? Answer me.” They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we should say, ‘From heaven;’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’ If we should say, ‘From men’”—they feared the people, for all held John to really be a prophet.
slang_text: Jesus goes, "I got one question for you first. Answer that, and I'll spill on my authority. John's baptism -- was that straight from heaven, or just some dude's idea?" So they huddled up, tryin' to figure their play: "If we say 'heaven,' he's gonna hit us with 'Then why didn't you believe him, dipsticks?' But if we say 'just some dude'..." -- they were scared of the crowd, 'cause everybody was totally sure John was a real prophet.

web_text:
They answered Jesus, “We don’t know.”
Jesus said to them,
“Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
slang_text: So they wimped out and told Jesus, "We got no clue." Jesus shot back, "Then talk to the hand -- I'm not tellin' you by what authority I do these things either."

web_text:
He began to speak to them in parables.
“A man planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a pit for the wine press, built a tower, rented it out to a farmer, and went into another country. When it was time, he sent a servant to the farmer to get from the farmer his share of the fruit of the vineyard. They took him, beat him, and sent him away empty. Again, he sent another servant to them; and they threw stones at him, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.
slang_text: Then Jesus busted out a parable for the crowd. "Check it -- this dude plants a bodacious vineyard, puts up a fence around it, digs out a pit for squeezing the grapes, builds a righteous tower, then rents the whole setup out to some farmers and bounces to another country. When harvest time rolls around, he sends a servant to collect his share of the fruit. Total bogus move by the farmers, though -- they grab the guy, whale on him, and send him back with nothin'. So he sends another servant, and these dipsticks straight-up stone him, bust his head open, and send him packing all shamed out."

web_text: Again he sent another; and they killed him; and many others, beating some, and killing some. Therefore still having one, his beloved son, he sent him last to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’ But those farmers said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’ They took him, killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
slang_text: "So he sends another one, and this time they flat-out kill him. And a bunch more after that -- beating some, killing some, no joke. Finally, he's only got one left: his own beloved son. He sends him last, figuring, 'They gotta respect my son.' But those grody farmers said to each other, 'Yo, this is the heir. Let's waste him and the whole inheritance is ours, like a boss.' So they grabbed him, killed him, and dumped him outside the vineyard. Way harsh."

web_text:
What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the farmers, and will give the vineyard to others. Haven’t you even read this Scripture:
‘The stone which the builders rejected,
the same was made the head of the corner. This was from the Lord,
it is marvelous in our eyes’?” They tried to seize him, but they feared the multitude; for they perceived that he spoke the parable against them. They left him, and went away.
slang_text: "So what's the vineyard owner gonna do about it? He's gonna roll up, waste those farmers, and hand the vineyard over to somebody else. Haven't you dudes even read the Scripture -- 'The stone the builders tossed out as garbage ended up the head cornerstone. That's the Lord's doing, and it's totally epic in our eyes'?" They wanted to grab him right then, but they were spazzin' about the crowd -- they knew good and well he was calling them out in that parable. So they bailed and left him alone.

web_text:
They sent some of the Pharisees and the Herodians to him, that they might trap him with words. When they had come, they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest, and don’t defer to anyone; for you aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not? Shall we give, or shall we not give?”
But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them,
“Why do you test me? Bring me a denarius, that I may see it.” They brought it.
He said to them,
“Whose is this image and inscription?”
They said to him, “Caesar’s.”
slang_text: So they sent some Pharisees and Herodians over to trap him with his own words -- total bogus setup. They come at him all smooth, "Teacher, we know you're straight-up honest, you don't play favorites or kiss up to anybody, you teach God's way for real. So is it legit to pay taxes to Caesar, or not? Should we pay up or should we not?" But Jesus wasn't trippin' -- he clocked their two-faced game right away and said, "Why you tryin' to test me? Bring me a coin so I can peep it." They brought one over. He goes, "Whose face and name is stamped on this thing?" They said, "Caesar's."

web_text:
Jesus answered them,
“Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
They marveled greatly at him. There came to him Sadducees, who say that there is no resurrection. They asked him, saying, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us, ‘If a man’s brother dies, and leaves a wife behind him, and leaves no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up offspring for his brother.’ There were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and dying left no offspring.
slang_text: Jesus answered them, "Give Caesar what's Caesar's, and give God what's God's." And they were totally blown away by that, like whoa. Then some Sadducees rolled up -- these are the dudes who say there's no such thing as resurrection, no way, no how. They tried to trap him too: "Teacher, Moses wrote that if a guy dies and leaves his wife behind with no kids, his brother's gotta marry her and raise up kids for him. Well, there were seven brothers. The first one got married and died with no kids."

web_text:
The second took her, and died, leaving no children behind him. The third likewise; and the seven took her and left no children. Last of all the woman also died. In the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be of them? For the seven had her as a wife.” Jesus answered them,
“Isn’t this because you are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God?
slang_text: "The second brother married her and died too, no kids. Same with the third. Matter of fact, all seven took her as a wife and none of 'em left any kids. Last of all, the woman died too. So here's the trippy question -- in the resurrection, whose wife is she gonna be? All seven had her!" Jesus wasn't havin' it. "Ain't it obvious you dipsticks are totally mistaken? You don't know your Scriptures and you don't know squat about God's power."

web_text: For when they will rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven. But about the dead, that they are raised; haven’t you read in the book of Moses, about the Bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are therefore badly mistaken.” One of the scribes came, and heard them questioning together. Knowing that he had answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the greatest of all?”
slang_text: "See, when people rise from the dead, there's no more marrying goin' on -- they're like angels up in heaven, fer sure. And about the dead being raised -- haven't you read in Moses' book, about the burning bush, how God said to him, 'I'm the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'? He's not the God of dead folks, dude, he's the God of the living. You are seriously trippin'." One of the scribes had been listening to the whole showdown, and seeing how Jesus schooled 'em good, he asked, "Out of all the commandments, which one's the greatest?"

web_text:
Jesus answered,
“The greatest is, ‘Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.’
This is the first commandment. The second is like this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
There is no other commandment greater than these.” The scribe said to him, “Truly, teacher, you have said well that he is one, and there is none other but he,
slang_text: Jesus answered, "The greatest is this: 'Listen up, Israel -- the Lord our God, the Lord is one. Love the Lord your God with your whole heart, your whole soul, your whole mind, and everything you've got.' That's number one. And the second is right there with it: 'Love your neighbor like you love yourself.' There's no commandment bigger than these two, period." The scribe said to him, "For real, Teacher, you nailed it -- he's one, and there's no other God besides him,

web_text:
and to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbor as himself, is more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.” When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him,
“You are not far from God’s Kingdom.”
No one dared ask him any question after that. Jesus responded, as he taught in the temple,
“How is it that the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David? For David himself said in the Holy Spirit,
‘The Lord said to my Lord,
“Sit at my right hand,
until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.”’
slang_text: and loving him with your whole heart, whole brain, whole soul, and all your strength, plus loving your neighbor like yourself -- that beats out all the burnt offerings and sacrifices put together, hands down." When Jesus saw the guy answered with some real wisdom, he told him, "You're not far from the Kingdom of God, dude." After that, nobody dared throw any more questions at him. Then Jesus, teaching away in the temple, said, "How come the scribes say the Christ is gotta be David's son? David himself, talking in the Holy Spirit, said: 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit right here at my right hand, till I stomp your enemies flat under your feet."'

web_text:
Therefore David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be his son?”

The common people heard him gladly. In his teaching he said to them,
“Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and to get greetings in the marketplaces, and the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts: those who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”
slang_text: "So David himself calls him 'Lord' -- how's that make him David's son, huh?" And the regular folks totally dug listening to him. In his teaching he told 'em, "Watch out for those scribes, strutting around in their long fancy robes, lovin' the special greetings in the marketplace, always grabbing the best seats at the synagogue and the prime spots at every feast. These posers chow down on widows' whole houses and then fake it with these long, showy prayers. They're gonna get slammed way harder for it come judgment."

web_text:
Jesus sat down opposite the treasury, and saw how the multitude cast money into the treasury. Many who were rich cast in much. A poor widow came, and she cast in two small brass coins, which equal a quadrans coin. He called his disciples to himself, and said to them,
“Most certainly I tell you, this poor widow gave more than all those who are giving into the treasury, for they all gave out of their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, gave all that she had to live on.”
slang_text: Jesus plopped down across from the offering box, just watching the crowd toss their cash in. Plenty of rich folks dropped in big wads of it. Then this poor widow shuffles up and drops in two tiny copper coins -- basically pocket change, worth almost nothing. Jesus called his disciples over and told them, straight up, "No lie, I'm tellin' you -- this poor widow gave more than every last one of those rich dudes. 'Cause they were just tossing in some of their leftover extra, but she, poor as she is, gave everything she had to live on. Every last cent."

web_text:
As he went out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, see what kind of stones and what kind of buildings!” Jesus said to him,
“Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone on another, which will not be thrown down.” As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately, “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are all about to be fulfilled?”
slang_text:
One of his disciples was walkin' out of the temple with him and goes, "Teacher, check out these stones, check out these buildings -- totally epic construction!" Jesus goes,

"You diggin' all these bodacious buildings? No way is one stone gonna stay stacked on another -- the whole thing's gettin' torn down, fer sure." So later he's chillin' on the Mount of Olives, right across from the temple, and Peter, James, John, and Andrew pull him aside privately and go, "Spill it -- when's this gonna go down? What's the sign that all this is about to happen?"

web_text:
Jesus, answering, began to tell them,
“Be careful that no one leads you astray. For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’
and will lead many astray. “When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don’t be troubled. For those must happen, but the end is not yet. For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places. There will be famines and troubles. These things are the beginning of birth pains.
slang_text:
Jesus laid it out for 'em straight up.

"Watch yourselves -- don't let nobody spazoid you into gettin' led astray. A bunch of dudes are gonna show up in my name, sayin' 'I'm the one!' and they're gonna lead a ton of people astray -- total bogus. When you hear about wars and rumors of wars, don't freak out. That stuff's gotta happen, but the end ain't here yet, no duh. Nation's gonna rise against nation, kingdom against kingdom. There's gonna be earthquakes poppin' off all over the place.  had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour. At the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying,
“Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?” which is, being interpreted,
“My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?” Some of those who stood by, when they heard it, said, “Behold, he is calling Elijah.” One ran, and filling a sponge full of vinegar, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to take him down.”
slang_text:
Around noon, darkness rolls in over the whole land, sticking around till three in the afternoon -- way harsh scene. At three, Jesus cries out loud,
"Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?" which means,
"My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?" Some folks standing there hear it and go, "Listen, he's calling for Elijah." Somebody runs, soaks a sponge in sour wine, sticks it on a reed, and holds it up for him to drink, saying, "Leave him be. Let's see if Elijah shows up to take him down."

web_text: Jesus cried out with a loud voice, and gave up the spirit. The veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom. When the centurion, who stood by opposite him, saw that he cried out like this and breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!” There were also women watching from afar, among whom were both Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;
slang_text: Jesus cries out loud one last time and breathes his last, gone. The curtain in the temple rips clean in two, top to bottom, no lie. The centurion standing across from him, seeing how he cried out and died like that, says, "This man really was the Son of God, for real." There were women watching from a distance too -- Mary Magdalene among 'em, and Mary the mother of James the younger and Joses, and Salome;

web_text: who, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and served him; and many other women who came up with him to Jerusalem. When evening had now come, because it was the Preparation Day, that is, the day before the Sabbath, Joseph of Arimathaea, a prominent council member who also himself was looking for God’s Kingdom, came. He boldly went in to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body. Pilate marveled if he were already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had been dead long.
slang_text: these women had followed him and looked after him back when he was in Galilee, plus plenty of others who'd come up with him to Jerusalem. By evening -- since it was Preparation Day, the day before the Sabbath -- Joseph of Arimathaea, a righteous council member who himself was waiting on God's Kingdom, works up the nerve to go to Pilate and ask for Jesus's body. Pilate's floored he'd already be dead, so he calls the centurion in and asks whether he'd really been dead that long.

web_text: When he found out from the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph. He bought a linen cloth, and taking him down, wound him in the linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb which had been cut out of a rock. He rolled a stone against the door of the tomb. Mary Magdalene and Mary, the mother of Joses, saw where he was laid.
slang_text: Once the centurion confirms it, Pilate lets Joseph take the body. Joseph buys a linen cloth, takes Jesus down, wraps him up in it, and lays him in a tomb cut straight outta the rock. Then he rolls a stone across the entrance, sealing it up tight. Mary Magdalene and Mary, the mother of Joses, watch and clock exactly where he's laid.

web_text: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, that they might come and anoint him. Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen. They were saying among themselves, “Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?” for it was very big. Looking up, they saw that the stone was rolled back.
slang_text: So the Sabbath finally wraps up, and Mary Magdalene, Mary the mom of James, and Salome go score some spices so they can head over and anoint him proper. Crack of dawn on the first day of the week, sun's barely up, and they're booking it to the tomb. Along the way they're all asking each other, "Okay, who's gonna roll that gnarly stone away from the tomb door for us?" 'cause that thing was hella massive. But they look up and -- oh snap -- the stone's already rolled back.

web_text: Entering into the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were amazed. He said to them, “Don’t be amazed. You seek Jesus, the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has risen. He is not here. Behold, the place where they laid him! But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He goes before you into Galilee. There you will see him, as he said to you.’” They went out, and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had come on them. They said nothing to anyone; for they were afraid.
slang_text: They duck into the tomb and there's this young dude sitting on the right side, decked out in a totally white robe, and they about lose it. He goes, "Don't have a cow. I know you're looking for Jesus, the guy from Nazareth, the one they nailed up on the cross. He's risen -- for real. He ain't here. Check it, right there's the spot where they laid him! Now bounce, go tell his disciples, and Peter too, 'He's heading to Galilee ahead of you. You'll see him there, just like he told you.'" They bolt outta that tomb, shaking like leaves, totally freaked, and they don't breathe a word to anybody 'cause they're scared stiff.

web_text: Now when he had risen early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons. She went and told those who had been with him, as they mourned and wept. When they heard that he was alive, and had been seen by her, they disbelieved. After these things he was revealed in another form to two of them, as they walked, on their way into the country.
slang_text: So after he rises up early that first day of the week, he shows himself first to Mary Magdalene -- the dudette he'd cast seven demons outta, back in the day. She goes and tells the guys who'd been hanging with Jesus, who are all busy moping and crying their eyes out. But when they hear he's alive and Mary saw him, they're all "I'm so sure" -- total disbelief, no way they're buying it. After that he shows up looking different to two of 'em while they're out walking, heading into the countryside.

web_text:
They went away and told it to the rest. They didn’t believe them, either. Afterward he was revealed to the eleven themselves as they sat at the table, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they didn’t believe those who had seen him after he had risen. He said to them,
“Go into all the world, and preach the Good News to the whole creation. He who believes and is baptized will be saved; but he who disbelieves will be condemned.
slang_text:
They go tell the rest of the crew, but nobody's buying that either. Then, later, he shows up to the eleven while they're chowing down at the table, and he lays into 'em for not believing and being so hard-headed, 'cause they wouldn't trust the folks who'd seen him alive. He goes,
"Bounce out into the whole world, and preach the Good News to every last creature out there. Whoever believes and gets baptized is gonna be saved; but whoever doesn't believe, that's bogus news -- they're condemned.

web_text: These signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak with new languages; they will take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it will in no way hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.” So then the Lord, after he had spoken to them, was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God. They went out, and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word by the signs that followed. Amen.
slang_text: Check out the totally rad signs that'll follow the believers: in my name they'll cast out demons; they'll speak in brand-new languages; they'll pick up snakes bare-handed; and if they drink somethin' deadly, it won't touch 'em, no joke; they'll lay hands on sick folks and those dudes'll get better." So then the Lord, after he lays all that on 'em, gets taken up into heaven -- totally epic -- and sits down at the right hand of God. Like a boss. And the disciples bounce out and preach everywhere, with the Lord right there working alongside 'em, backing up the word with the righteous signs that followed. Amen, dudes.
